item.title: Ex-Lt Gen Masud put on 3-day fresh remand
item.description: A metropolitan magistrate court in Dhaka on Saturday placed former Feni-3 lawmaker and retired lieutenant general Masud Uddin Chowdhury on a three-day fresh remand in a case filed with Paltan police station over allegations of human trafficking and embezzlement....Diaspora community news

item.title: BSF returns Bangladeshi’s body 11 months after detention
item.description: The Indian Border Security Force has returned a Bangladesh national’s body on Friday afternoon 11 months after the force had detained him along Shahanabad border area at Ranishankail upazila in Thakurgaon....

item.title: No respite from long queues for fuel
item.description: The cabinet committee on government purchase on Saturday approved two proposals to procure one lakh tonnes of diesel and two cargoes of liquefied natural gas amid persistently high demand for petroleum products, as consumers continued to......

item.title: Rajshahi 2nd worst-hit by measles after Dhaka
item.description: Rajshahi division has emerged as the second worst-hit region after Dhaka in the ongoing measles outbreak, while the government begins today an emergency measles vaccination drive in 30 hotspot upazilas across Bangladesh....Maps

item.title: Most commodities crossing Hormuz linked to Iran: AFP
item.description: Sixty percent of commodity-bearing ships crossing the Strait of Hormuz since the start of the Middle East war have either come from Iran or were heading there, an AFP analysis of maritime data showed......

item.title: Food commodity prices up on Mideast war: FAO
item.description: The war in the Middle East has pushed food commodity prices higher due to higher energy and fertiliser costs, the UN’s food agency said Friday......

item.title: EU under pressure as fertiliser costs soar on Middle East war
item.description: With the war in the Middle East sending global fertiliser prices soaring, the EU is coming under pressure from farmers and some member states to take action......

item.title: NZ electric vehicle sales soar
item.description: Electric vehicle sales in New Zealand more than tripled in March according to official government figures released this week......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: European drivers choke on rising diesel prices
item.description: The price of diesel fuel has rocketed by more than 30 percent across Europe since the start of the Middle East war highlighting the continent’s dependence on imported energy and the lingering prominence of diesel on the car market......

item.title: Netflix may have to reimburse Italian subscription
item.description: US streaming giant Netflix may have to reimburse hundreds of millions of euros to its Italian customers after a court ruling, according to a judgement published Friday by a consumer association......

item.title: Turkey annual inflation down slightly in Mar
item.description: Turkey’s annual inflation rate slowed slightly to 30.9 per cent in March, from 31.5 in February, official figures showed on Friday......

item.title: Five EU nations urge tax on energy firms’ windfall profits
item.description: Spain and four other European Union nations have called for a tax on windfall profits of energy companies in response to rising fuel prices due to the Middle East war, Spanish Economy Minister Carlos Cuerpo said Saturday......

item.title: French, Japanese ships cross Hormuz
item.description: One French- and another Japanese-owned vessel are among a handful of vessels to have crossed the war-torn Strait of Hormuz, maritime tracking data showed Friday......

item.title: Italy extends fuel excise tax cuts for month
item.description: Italy’s government has extended by a month a temporary cut in fuel excise taxes that were due to expire on April 6, the economy minister said Friday......

item.title: Agrani Bank launches foundation course
item.description: A 30-working-day foundation course for the 96th and 97th batches of officers (Cash) has been inaugurated on Saturday by the Agrani Bank Training Institute of Agrani Bank PLC......

item.description: Centre for the Rehabilitation of the Paralysed, in partnership with MetLife Foundation, has recently provided training, certification, and financial and technical assistance to 40 persons with disabilities to help them start small businesses and achieve financial independence......

item.title: Midland Bank organises leadership development workshop
item.description: Midland Bank organised a workshop titled ‘Leadership for Women at Work’ to enhance the professional skills and leadership capabilities of its female employees......

item.title: BRAC Bank to provide employee banking for InterContinental
item.description: BRAC Bank recently partnered with InterContinental Dhaka to deliver a rewarding and seamless employee banking experience......

item.title: BNP standing committee prioritises next national council
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party, at a standing committee meeting on Saturday, underscored the need to hold its next national council at the earliest possible time as part of efforts to strengthen the party’s organisational structure......Bangladesh travel guide

item.description: RFL Group recently launched a showroom of its electric scooter brand ‘RYDO’ at Rupayan Shopping Square in Bashundhara Residential Area of the capital......

item.title: bKash enables easy instalment payments for Metal Motors
item.description: Farmers can now pay instalments for mechanised agricultural vehicles purchased from Metal Motors at nearby bKash agent points......Maps

item.title: What comes next as Trump deadline expires
item.description: IN MARCH 2025, the question was whether diplomacy’s collapse would lead to a US strike to preserve the regional status quo or a desperate nuclear reveal by Tehran to deter it. By June 22, the dice had rolled. The United States bombed Iran. Later, an Israeli surprise attack took place on June 12 designed to decapitate Iran’s defence, Tehran’s unexpected resilience and......

item.title: Untangling confusion over law, constitutional authority
item.description: A RECENT remark in the parliament by the home minister, Mr. Salahuddin Ahmed, has reignited a fundamental constitutional debate. Referring to the July National Charter Implementation Order, he stated: This order is neither an ordinance nor a law — it is something in between.’......

item.title: Cyber sovereignty v civil liberty
item.description: BANGLADESH’S digital legal architecture reveals how authoritarian legalism can hollow out constitutional safeguards while preserving the outward appearance of legality. Article 39 of the Constitution promises freedom of speech and of the press. Yet that promise sits alongside a dense web of statutes that steadily recast dissent as a threat to security rather than a feature......

item.title: Time to cut tax rates, widen base
item.description: ALTHOUGH Bangladesh is progressing towards becoming a middle-income country, it has not yet achieved the expected success in domestic revenue mobilisation. The tax-to-GDP ratio remains below 7 per cent, which is extremely low by global standards and a matter of serious concern for a developing economy. This reality indicates that the current tax system fails to......

item.title: Footpath eviction demands sustainable planning
item.description: THE eviction drives that the city police are conducting to reclaim footpaths are welcome in that pedestrians need to use the footpath and vehicles should run without having to face pedestrians on the road. But, it has already been reported that footpaths in areas such as Indira Road, Bangla Motor, Moghbazar and the surroundings of Dhaka Medical College Hospital......Maps

item.title: Unilateral austerity measures ineffective, risky
item.description: THE austerity measures aimed at managing the looming crisis caused by the US-Israel war on Iran appear short-sighted and they risk slowing the economy. The war has disrupted fuel flows through the Strait of Hormuz, responsible for about 20 per cent of global oil transit, triggering what analysts describe as a historic supply shock. In this context, the government has adopted......

item.title: Curbing tobacco use to protect human resources urged
item.description: Chief whip Nurul Islam Moni on Saturday said that the government was committed to curbing tobacco use to safeguard the country’s human resources....

item.title: No arrest after man shot dead over sand lifting
item.description: The police have yet to make any arrests, more than 24 hours after a man was shot dead and another injured in a dispute over illegal sand lifting in the Alokdia Char area at Shibaloy in Manikganj on Friday evening....

item.title: College teacher mugged in Sylhet
item.description: Miscreants snatched away Tk 20,000 of a college teacher from Sylhet on Saturday afternoon after brandishing a knife around the neck of his three-year old son....

item.title: Energy minister seeks business support for uninterrupted power supply during summer, irrigation season
item.description: Power, energy and mineral resources minister Iqbal Hasan Mahmood Tuku on Saturday said that the government’s decisions would be implemented with support from business leaders to ensure uninterrupted electricity supply during the summer and irrigation season....

item.description: Water resources minister Md Shahiduddin Chowdhury Anee on Saturday said that people in the coastal regions were surviving by fighting droughts, floods and various natural disasters, and the government is working to ensure they are no longer neglected or deprived....

item.description: Law justice and parliamentary affairs minister Md Asaduzzaman on Saturday said that the non-passage of the referendum ordinance had no connection with implementing the July Charter....

item.description: Local administration imposed Section 144 of the Code of Criminal Procedure restricting public gathering on Saturday afternoon after at least 20 people had been injured in a clash between two groups of villagers at Sunamganj Sadar upazila....

item.description: US president Donald Trump asked Congress on Friday for $152 million to begin rebuilding the notorious Alcatraz prison, pressing ahead with his vision to return the former island lockup to active use....

item.description: The National Citizen Party called on former chief adviser Muhammad Yunus and other advisers to the previous interim government to denounce the current government’s moves to repeal several ordinances introduced during the interim government....

item.description: Arsenal suffered a shock FA Cup quarter-final exit as the Premier League leaders crashed to an embarrassing 2-1 defeat against second tier Southampton on Saturday....

item.title: Lewandowski claims leaders Barca vital Liga win at Atletico
item.description:
Robert Lewandowski shouldered home a dramatic late winner for Barcelona as they edged Atletico Madrid 2-1 on Saturday to move seven points clear at the top of La Liga after Real Madrid slipped up.

item.description: Bangladesh Bank has prepared a draft guideline to launch a formal Islamic Interbank Money Market, setting clear rules for unsecured, Shariah-compliant short-term liquidity transactions among Islamic banks and Islamic windows of conventional banks......

item.description: Nepalese billionaire industrialist and chairman of CG Corp Global, Binod K. Chaudhary, on Saturday said Bangladesh and Nepal must strengthen bilateral trade and economic cooperation......

item.title: Starlink sees slow uptake in Bangladesh
item.description: Satellite internet service provider Starlink has not achieved significant growth in Bangladesh, attracting only 3,990 subscribers in the nine months since its launch, despite offering high-speed connectivity, according to data from the Bangladesh Telecommunication Regulatory Commission as of February 2026......

item.description: Finance minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury on Saturday said that Bangladesh would work with the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB) to address the ongoing energy crisis and support future development priorities......

item.description: The Bangladesh Bank has set new timings for office hours for all finance companies effective from today, in order to conserve fuel and electricity across the country......

item.description: The benchmark index of the Dhaka Stock Exchange (DSE) declined by 148 points over the week as persistent selling pressure dominated four out of five trading sessions, eroding market capitalisation......Geographic Reference

item.description: Sri Lanka is struggling to prevent a repeat of its spectacular economic collapse four years ago, as the prolonged Middle East war compounds the fallout from a deadly cyclone in November......

item.description: Beneath the quiet agricultural landscape of Pirganj upazila in Rangpur lies a long-standing but largely untapped story of coal reserves and iron-rich mineral deposits, now drawing renewed attention from policymakers and experts......

item.description: Thailand’s prime minister slammed on Friday oil traders for ‘excessive profiteering’, blaming those stockpiling fuel or smuggling it abroad for shortages that have driven prices steadily higher......

item.description: Indian high commissioner to Bangladesh Pranay Verma has said that art remains a powerful medium connecting the people of Bangladesh and India, fostering closer cultural bonds between the two neighbours....Diaspora community news

item.description: Several thousand people marched through the streets of Paris’ largest suburb on Saturday to denounce racism after the town’s newly elected black mayor was subject to disparaging comments on social media and national television shows....

item.description: Dhaka Metropolitan Police continued its eviction drive in several areas of the capital for the third day on Saturday, jailing 13 people for varying terms and realising Tk 3,70,500 in fines, according to a DMP press release....Geographic Reference

item.description: President Donald Trump said on Saturday that Tehran had 48 hours left to cut a deal or face ‘all Hell’, as US and Iranian forces scrambled to find a downed American airman....
